android.versionNote: Updated 2026-07-11 · Android 13+ notification/media permissions and Android 14+ behavior changes are called out where relevant.
android.permissionVersionNote: Android 13+: notification and granular media permissions. Android 14+: selected photos access and foreground-service type restrictions. Request permissions only at the point of use.
android.adbTroubleshooting: unauthorized → confirm the RSA prompt; offline → reconnect or restart adb; no devices → check USB debugging, cable and drivers.
android.composeMaterialNote: Material 3 is the default for new Compose apps. Basic layout primitives such as Box and Row are framework components; Material widgets are marked M3.

unitconvert.invalid: Enter a valid number; decimals and scientific notation are supported
unitconvert.invalidRadix: Enter an integer valid for this base; 0b, 0o and 0x prefixes and underscore separators are supported
unitconvert.invalidBeaufort: Beaufort force must be an integer from 0 to 12
unitconvert.invalidFuel: Fuel consumption must be non-negative and efficiency units must be greater than 0
unitconvert.zeroFuel: 0 L/100 km represents infinite fuel efficiency
unitconvert.machNote: Mach uses the standard speed of sound at sea level and 15°C: 340.29 m/s
unitconvert.absoluteZero: The result is below absolute zero and has mathematical meaning only
unitconvert.windFromLevel: Force {level} covers {range}; other speeds use the range midpoint
unitconvert.windFromLevelOpen: Force {level} covers {range}; the open-ended range uses its lower bound for other speeds

notes.radix: Convert integers among binary, octal, decimal and hexadecimal; negative values, common prefixes and arbitrary precision are supported.
notes.length: Convert metric, imperial and traditional Chinese length units in real time.
notes.area: Includes mu, hectares and acres; 1 mu equals 2000/3 square meters.
notes.volume: US and Imperial fluid ounces are distinct from mass ounces.
notes.mass: Includes jin, liang, pounds and avoirdupois ounces; oz here measures mass.
notes.speed: Includes knots and Mach; Mach uses a standard speed-of-sound approximation.
notes.temperature: Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin and Rankine use their proper scale formulas.
notes.wind: Beaufort force is a speed range; forces 0–11 use the midpoint, while open-ended force 12 uses its lower bound.
notes.data: KB, MB, GB and TB use powers of 1000; KiB, MiB, GiB and TiB use powers of 1024.
notes.time: Only fixed durations are included; months and years vary in length and are omitted.
notes.pressure: mmHg and Torr are nearly equal but use slightly different definitions.
notes.energy: cal is the thermochemical calorie; food-label Calories usually mean kcal.
notes.power: PS is metric horsepower and hp is mechanical horsepower; their values differ.
notes.angle: Convert radians, degrees, gradians, turns, arcminutes and arcseconds.
notes.flow: Includes metric, US, Imperial and HVAC-standard CFM flow rates.
notes.cooking: A US cup is about 236.59 mL while a metric cup is 250 mL; spoons also differ.
notes.fuel: Lower L/100 km is better; higher km/L or mpg is better. US and Imperial mpg use different gallons.

text.stats: Characters {chars} · No whitespace {charsNoSpace} · Lines {lines} · Words {words} · UTF-8 bytes {bytes}

regex.engineNote: PCRE-only recursion, atomic groups and many inline modifiers are unsupported. RE2 avoids backtracking but rejects backreferences and lookbehind.
regex.riskBacktracking: Nested quantifiers may cause catastrophic backtracking on long input
regex.riskAmbiguous: Repeated alternation may trigger excessive backtracking
regex.explainAnchored: Anchors constrain matches to the start or end
regex.explainClasses: Character classes match groups of characters
regex.explainCaptures: Numbered capture groups are shown as $1, $2
regex.explainLiteral: Scans the input from left to right
regex.zeroLengthHint: Found {count} zero-length matches; they consume no characters and require loop guards
regex.noObviousRisk: No obvious high-risk backtracking structure detected

tax.title: China Tax Calculator
tax.subtitle: Real-time calculation · China IIT · Year-end bonus comparison
tax.assumptionNote: Estimate for a mainland China resident with the same salary and deductions each month, using cumulative withholding.
tax.rateNote: Default rates are common examples only; local rates and contribution base caps vary.
tax.averageNote: Monthly values are annual averages. Actual withholding changes as cumulative taxable income crosses brackets.
tax.scheduleNote: Assumes unchanged salary and deductions. Monthly withholding equals cumulative tax due minus tax already withheld.
tax.bracketNote: Only the portion within each bracket is taxed at that bracket's rate; the highest rate does not apply to all income.
tax.guideIntro: Salary IIT is ultimately calculated on annual comprehensive income. Employers withhold tax monthly on a cumulative basis, followed by annual reconciliation with refunds or additional payment.
tax.guideGrossMeaning: Salary before employee contributions and individual income tax are deducted.
tax.guideGrossFormula: Annual salary income = sum of monthly gross salaries
tax.guideContributionMeaning: Employee pension, medical, unemployment insurance, and housing fund contributions are deductible before IIT. Employer contributions are separate and are not deducted from the employee's gross salary.
tax.guideContributionFormula: Employee contribution = local contribution base × employee rate
tax.guideBasicMeaning: The standard basic deduction is currently CNY 5,000 per month or CNY 60,000 per year. Salary above CNY 5,000 is not taxed in full.
tax.guideSpecialMeaning: Eligible deductions include children's education, continuing education, serious illness medical costs, mortgage interest, rent, elderly care, and care for children under age three.
tax.guideSpecialFormula: Annual special deductions = sum of eligible monthly deductions
tax.guideTaxableMeaning: The amount used with the annual seven-bracket rate table; it is not the same as gross salary.
tax.guideTaxableFormula: Annual income − 60,000 − employee contributions − special deductions, etc.
tax.guideTaxMeaning: Seven marginal brackets apply. Only the portion inside a bracket is taxed at that bracket's rate.
tax.guideTaxFormula: Sum each bracket amount × its rate, or taxable income × rate − quick deduction
tax.contributionIntro: The rates below are common employee examples used by this calculator, not nationwide fixed rates.
tax.pensionGuide: An 8% employee rate is common for basic pension insurance; employers normally make a separate employer contribution.
tax.medicalGuide: An employee rate around 2% is common; some locations add a fixed amount or supplementary medical contribution.
tax.unemploymentGuide: Employee rates vary by location; 0.5% is only a common example.
tax.housingGuide: Common rates range from 5% to 12%. Employers usually contribute at the same rate; account funds belong to the employee but withdrawal and loan rules are local.
tax.exampleIntro: This example updates with the salary, deductions, and rates entered above.
tax.guideProgressiveFormula: Sum the annual marginal tax from each used bracket
tax.progressiveExample: Tax brackets used by the current annual taxable income:
tax.guideDisclaimer: Note: this calculator estimates employee contributions by applying rates directly to monthly salary. Actual contributions use local bases and upper/lower caps. Bonuses, service income, equity incentives, multiple employers, or partial-year employment may require separate treatment.

mortgage.title: Mortgage Calculator
mortgage.referenceNote: Use the rate in your loan contract. LPR is a pricing reference and is not necessarily your final mortgage rate.

ios.versionNote: Updated 2026-07-11 · Minimum versions are shown per API; verify availability against the linked Apple documentation.
ios.modernSwiftNote: async/await and actors (iOS 13+), Observation and SwiftData (iOS 17+), plus strict concurrency checking in Swift 6.
ios.reviewGuidanceNote: Permission descriptions must explain the concrete user benefit. Example: “Allow camera access to scan QR codes for device setup.” Generic text such as “App needs camera” may be rejected.
ios.deviceSourceNote: Source: Apple device compatibility and product specifications · Updated 2026-07-11.

jwt.invalidHeader: Header decode failed — not valid Base64URL
jwt.invalidPayload: Payload decode failed — not valid Base64URL
jwt.decodedNotVerified: Decoded only; signature has not been verified
jwt.warningNoExpiry: Missing exp; the token has no explicit expiry
jwt.warningNoIssuedAt: Missing iat; issue time cannot be assessed
jwt.warningFutureIssuedAt: iat is later than the current time
jwt.warningLongLifetime: Lifetime exceeds 30 days; confirm this is intentional
jwt.warningInvalidWindow: nbf is later than or equal to exp
jwt.warningUnsafeAlgorithm: Signing algorithm is empty or none

focus.headline: Eyes ready?
focus.subtitle: Start at 1 and find every number in order. The positions are shuffled each round.
focus.readyHint: The timer starts when you do
focus.completedHint: You found every number in the right order
focus.historyEmpty: Finish your first round and the score will be saved in this browser.
howSteps: Choose a level and press Start challenge. The timer begins immediately.
Click the numbers in order from 1 through the final number in the grid.
Keep a steady rhythm. A wrong tap does not end the game, but it counts as a mistake.
focus.scienceTitle: Treat it as a visual-search game
focus.scienceNote: Schulte Grids practice sequential scanning, visual search and staying engaged with the current task. Getting faster mainly shows that you are improving at this kind of task; it does not mean intelligence or attention in every setting will improve.
